• 2015 Jun--2021 Aug Yuan Zhao Ph.D. defense: 2016 Aug
    Ph.D. student in Applied Mathematics and Statistics Stony Brook University (co-advisor: Hongshik Ahn)
    Thesis: Log-linear Model Based Tree and Latent Variable Model for Count Data
    Postdoctoral Associate
    currently ML Research Scientist at NIMH
  • 2016 Jun--2017 Dec David Hocker
    Postdoctoral Associate
    currently Postdoc at NYU
  • 2017 Jun--2019 Dec Logan Becker
    Master student in Program in Neuroscience
    Thesis: The Linear-Nonlinear Weibull Model
  • 2017 Aug--2025 Sep Piotr A. Sokół Ph.D. defense: 2023 May
    Supports: Neural Dynamics Laboratory (internal funding), National Institutes of Health (NIH) 9RF1DA056404-04
    Ph.D. student in Program in Neuroscience
    Thesis: Geometry of learning and representation in neural networks
    Short-term Postdoctoral Fellow
    Short-term Postdoctoral Fellow
  • 2018 Jan--2019 Aug Sima Mofakham
    Postdoctoral Associate (co-advisor: Charles Mikell)
    currently Assistant Professor in Dept. Neurosurgery, Stony Brook Medicine
  • 2018 Jan--2022 Apr Josue Nassar Ph.D. defense: 2022 Apr
    Ph.D. student in Electrical and Computer Engineering (co-advisor: Monica Bugallo)
    Thesis: Bayesian Machine Learning for Analyzing and Controlling Neural Populations
    Honors: President's awards to Distinguished Doctoral Student
  • 2018 Jun--2023 May Ian Jordan Ph.D. defense: 2022 Apr
    Ph.D. student in Applied Mathematics and Statistics
    Thesis: Metastable Dynamics Underlying Neural Computation
    Postdoctoral Associate
    currently CEO of RyvivyR
  • 2019 Sep--present Matthew Dowling Ph.D. defense: 2023 Dec
    Supports: Neural Dynamics Laboratory (internal funding)
    Ph.D. student in Electrical and Computer Engineering (co-advisor: Monica Bugallo)
    Thesis: Approximate Bayesian Inference for State-space Models of Neural Dynamics
    Postdoctoral Fellow
  • 2020 Jan--2020 Dec Diego Arribas
    Visiting Scholar Biomedicine Research Institute of Buenos Aires, Argentina
  • 2020 Jun--2025 Dec Ayesha Vermani Ph.D. defense: 2025 Oct
    Supports: PRR - Center for Responsible AI
    Ph.D. student in INDP Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown
    Thesis: Identifying Invariant Representations underlying Neural Computation
    Postdoctoral Fellow

    Master student in Program in Neuroscience Stony Brook University
    Thesis: Exploring the expressive power of latent variable models
    currently Ph.D. student at Boston University

    Supports: Neural Dynamics Laboratory (internal funding)
    Ph.D. student in INDP Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown
    Project: An interpretable language for robust neural computation

    Ph.D. student in INDP Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown
